new_feedback.issue: The connection pip is grey (#3A4654) with a small ✓ glyph while the label still reads 'Live updates · connected'. Happy and empty states render the identical 'connected' label with a GREEN pip (#57C08A). A grey dot next to 'connected' reads as degraded/unknown, so the same transport state is signalled three different ways across the three frames. For an operator who scans the top-right connection indicator the way they scan Vercel/Datadog, an inconsistent pip color for an unchanged state is a false signal.
new_feedback.change: If the SSE transport is still connected in the error frame, use the same green pip (#57C08A with the 0 0 0 3px #57C08A22 ring) as the other two states. Reserve grey/amber for an actually-degraded transport, and if you show degraded, change the label too (e.g. 'reconnecting') so color and text agree.

new_feedback.element: Empty-state right rail: 'Not yet measured' tile + RUN SUMMARY dashed chips (lines 334-344)
new_feedback.issue: The empty frame stacks four 1px-dashed #3A4654 placeholders in the right rail (three 'not picked / not pasted / not set' chips plus the dashed 'Not yet measured' tile), and the YAML drop zone is also dashed. Four-plus dashed outlines in one column is border-on-border chrome with low data-to-chrome ratio; the rail reads as a wireframe rather than a launcher left open on a second monitor. Worsens the density the persona wants.
new_feedback.change: Demote the three summary placeholders from bordered dashed chips to borderless muted text (e.g. em-dash or 'not picked' in #95A1AE, no box), and keep ONE dashed affordance per region (the YAML drop zone). Let the single 'Not yet measured' tile carry the empty signal in the rail instead of repeating a dashed treatment four times.
